criteria.label: Create an OO scatter plot with axis labels, title, legend, and tight_layout.
criteria.example: On Iris columns 0 and 1, fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6,4)); loop species with ax.scatter and label=; ax.set_xlabel/set_ylabel; ax.legend(); fig.tight_layout().

criteria.label: Plot train and validation loss on one axes with distinguishable labeled series.
criteria.example: Given epoch-indexed train_loss and val_loss lists, ax.plot both with label='Train' and label='Val', ax.legend(), ylabel='Loss'.

criteria.label: Build a 2×2 subplot grid of histograms for four numeric features.
criteria.example: fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(8,6)); for each ax and column, ax.hist(df[col], bins=30); per-ax set_title(col).

criteria.label: Save a figure to PNG with readable resolution and no clipped labels.
criteria.example: After plotting, fig.tight_layout(); fig.savefig('eda_hist.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight').

criteria.label: Explain scope: what Matplotlib owns vs Seaborn and embedding visualization.
criteria.example: State that ax.scatter syntax and Figure/Axes are here; Seaborn owns statistical defaults; embedding viz owns coordinate-plot interpretation—not algorithm tuning.
